prompts.label: Analyze repo
prompts.purpose: Baseline governed-work intake covering semantic understanding, clarification, permission, Governor planning, and plan-ready state.
assertions: Prompt is treated as governed work, not casual dialogue.
Permission is requested before planning or execution.
Plan-ready state is authoritative before execute actions appear.

prompts.label: Architecture analysis
prompts.prompt: Analyze the repo. Focus on architecture, structure, and subsystem boundaries.
prompts.purpose: Exercises broad but bounded analysis and checks that Corgi preserves the user's narrowed scope.
assertions: Scope remains architecture, structure, and subsystem boundaries.
Governor plan names likely repo areas without creating dispatch truth.
Executor does not start before Execute permission.

prompts.label: Develop feature
prompts.purpose: Covers implementation-style work intent that should stay governed and permission-gated.
assertions: Feature wording is not rejected merely because it is terse.
Planning remains separate from dispatch truth.
Execution remains unavailable until Execute permission.

prompts.label: Question-shaped work
prompts.prompt: can we develop the internet feature?
prompts.purpose: Checks that natural question phrasing can still be interpreted as possible governed work.
assertions: Question-shaped work is not forced through a keyword shortcut.
Ambiguity becomes clarification or a safe Governor dialogue response.
No permission scope changes silently.

prompts.label: Progress question
prompts.purpose: Exercises read-only Governor dialogue and progress/status presentation.
assertions: Progress stays read-only.
No intake, dispatch, or plan-ready state is created.
Governor output is shown without leaking internal proposal fields.

prompts.label: Greeting
prompts.purpose: Fast smoke test for lightweight dialogue and first-turn session handling.
assertions: First-turn submit may omit session_ref.
Greeting does not create dispatch truth.
Governor reply remains concise.

prompts.label: Review implementation
prompts.prompt: review the implementation for bugs, risks, and missing tests
prompts.purpose: Exercises read-heavy review intent and later Reviewer handoff expectations.
assertions: Review intent remains bounded and read-oriented until execution is explicitly authorized.
Reviewer is not treated as a writer.
Findings should prioritize bugs, risks, and missing tests.

prompts.label: Mixed stop and work
prompts.purpose: Checks fail-closed behavior for mixed control and work intent.
assertions: Mixed stop and new work should not be silently split.
No dispatch truth is created from a mixed control turn.
User-facing copy should ask for a clearer single intent.

prompts.label: Permission follow-up
prompts.purpose: Covers ambiguous follow-up language while a permission choice is active.
assertions: Free text does not silently click permission buttons.
Permission scope changes only through authoritative permission actions.
Ambiguous follow-up remains safe.
